root_text.an7.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an7.1:2.1: “Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an7.1:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu lābhakāmo ca hoti, sakkārakāmo ca hoti, anavaññattikāmo ca hoti, ahiriko ca hoti, anottappī ca, pāpiccho ca, micchādiṭṭhi ca.
root_text.an7.1:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an7.1:3.1: Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti, manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an7.1:3.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na lābhakāmo ca hoti, na sakkārakāmo ca hoti, na anavaññattikāmo ca hoti, hirīmā ca hoti, ottappī ca, appiccho ca, sammādiṭṭhi ca.
root_text.an7.1:3.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an7.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an7.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an7.1:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with seven qualities is disliked and disapproved by their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an7.1:2.3: It’s when a mendicant desires material things, honor, and status. They lack conscience and prudence. They have corrupt wishes and wrong view.
translation_text.an7.1:2.4: A mendicant with these seven qualities is disliked and disapproved by their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an7.1:3.1: A mendicant with seven qualities is liked and approved by their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an7.1:3.3: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t desire material things, honor, and status. They have conscience and prudence. They have few desires and right view.
translation_text.an7.1:3.4: A mendicant with these seven qualities is liked and approved by their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”
